description: Repeated deployment queries should learn which facts matter

memories.content: Production uses blue-green deployment
The office wifi password is fish123
CI pipeline runs on GitHub Actions
Lunch is at 12:30
Rollback procedure: revert last commit, redeploy

gold_answer: blue-green deployment with rollback via revert and redeploy

description: Database connection queries should prefer technical config over noise

memories.content: Database host is db.prod.internal:5432
Team celebrates birthdays on Fridays
DB connection pool size is 20
Coffee is free in the kitchen
DB user is app_user, password in Vault at secret/db

query: How do I connect to the database?
gold_answer: DB host db.prod.internal:5432, pool size 20, credentials in Vault

description: Security incident response: relevant procedures rise above noise

memories.content: On security breach: isolate the affected node immediately
The office has a standing desk policy
Security incidents must be reported to security@company.com
Monthly newsletter goes out on the 1st
Incident log is maintained in Jira under SEC project
Free snacks are in the pantry

query: What do I do during a security incident?
gold_answer: isolate node, report to security@company.com, log in Jira SEC project

description: API key rotation: technical procedures outrank social noise

memories.content: API keys expire every 90 days
Key rotation script: scripts/rotate_keys.sh
The parking lot is at B2 level
Old keys are invalidated 24h after rotation
Next team outing is bowling night

query: How do I rotate API keys?
gold_answer: keys expire every 90 days, run scripts/rotate_keys.sh, old keys invalidated after 24h

description: On-call escalation: learn which escalation paths matter

memories.content: Primary on-call is pinged via PagerDuty
Escalation path: engineer -> lead -> VP Engineering
PagerDuty team ID: team_prod_123
The kitchen has oat milk now
SLA for P1 incidents is 15 minutes response

query: How do I escalate a production incident?
gold_answer: PagerDuty primary on-call, escalate engineer->lead->VP, P1 SLA is 15 minutes

description: Code review process: technical workflow facts surface above trivia

memories.content: PRs require two approvals before merge
CI must be green before merging
The printer is on the 3rd floor
Use squash-merge to keep history clean
Free coffee tokens distributed monthly
Draft PRs are allowed and encouraged for early feedback

query: What is the code review and merge process?
gold_answer: two approvals, CI green, squash-merge, draft PRs for early feedback

description: Architecture decisions: important design facts outrank fluff

memories.content: Service mesh uses Istio for inter-service communication
All services emit metrics to Prometheus
The CEO prefers morning meetings
Circuit breaker pattern is required for external calls
Inter-service auth uses JWT with 1h TTL

query: How do services communicate and authenticate?
gold_answer: Istio service mesh, circuit breaker for external calls, JWT auth with 1h TTL

description: Testing requirements: relevant test policy rises above social facts

memories.content: Unit test coverage must be >= 80%
Integration tests run in Docker Compose environment
Happy hour is every last Friday of the month
Flaky tests must be quarantined and fixed within one sprint
Performance tests run nightly on staging
The espresso machine was repaired last week

query: What are the testing requirements for a new feature?
gold_answer: 80% unit coverage, integration in Docker, quarantine flaky tests, nightly perf tests

description: Logging standards: technical log requirements beat irrelevant facts

memories.content: Logs must use structured JSON format
Log level should be INFO in production, DEBUG only in dev
There is a foosball table in the games room
Sensitive data (PII, secrets) must never appear in logs
Logs are shipped to Datadog with 30-day retention

gold_answer: structured JSON, INFO in prod, no PII in logs, shipped to Datadog with 30-day retention

description: Secrets management: vault/secrets facts rank above social noise

memories.content: All secrets stored in HashiCorp Vault at vault.internal
Applications access secrets via VAULT_TOKEN env variable
Team trivia night is every Wednesday
Never hardcode secrets in source code or configs
Secret rotation triggers a graceful restart of affected services

gold_answer: Vault at vault.internal, use VAULT_TOKEN, never hardcode, rotation triggers graceful restart

description: Feature flags: relevant flag system docs surface over noise

memories.content: Feature flags managed in LaunchDarkly
New features must be wrapped in a feature flag for gradual rollout
The office dog is named Biscuit
Flag cleanup: remove flags within 2 weeks of full rollout
Emergency kill switch available in LaunchDarkly dashboard

query: How do we use feature flags?
gold_answer: LaunchDarkly, wrap new features, cleanup 2 weeks after full rollout, emergency kill switch

description: Data migration procedures: procedural steps outrank trivia

memories.content: Database migrations use Alembic, run with alembic upgrade head
Always take a DB snapshot before running migrations in prod
The team uses Notion for documentation
Migrations are tested on staging for 24h before prod
There is a whiteboard in meeting room A

query: How do I run a database migration?
gold_answer: Alembic upgrade head, snapshot DB first, test on staging for 24h before prod

description: Monitoring and alerting: ops knowledge beats social chatter

memories.content: Grafana dashboards at grafana.internal:3000
Alerts are routed via PagerDuty based on severity
Error rate > 1% triggers a P1 alert automatically
The office has bean bags in the lounge
Runbooks for each alert are in the ops wiki

query: How do I monitor production and respond to alerts?
gold_answer: Grafana at grafana.internal, PagerDuty routing, P1 on error rate > 1%, runbooks in ops wiki
